purpose: Capture only useful platform gaps that are not already covered by security, domain cleanup, native worker, Blender, FreeCAD, KiCad or later KRITIS/Enterprise tracks. This is not a management roadmap and not a feature wishlist.
scope_rules: Do not duplicate todo.kritis.json, todo.blender.json, todo.freecad.json or todo.kicad.json, and avoid reintroducing archived Security/Domain/Native-Worker tracks unless they are explicitly restored.
Only include items that improve installability, operability, controlled integrations or dogfooding after the core tracks are stable.
Avoid Medusa features such as visual flow builders, full LSP server rewrites or large editor refactors unless explicitly promoted later.
All integrations must remain Hub-controlled, policy-gated, auditable and compatible with thin clients.
success_criteria: Ananta can be initialized on a local machine with a clear runtime profile without requiring Docker by default.
MCP integration is available in a read-only/default-deny Hub-controlled mode before any write/admin tool access is enabled.
Pull-request review automation can be enabled only after native worker and release gates are stable.
External tools and setup modes are represented as capability/profile choices rather than hard-coded client or worker behavior.
Deferred high-complexity ideas remain explicitly frozen instead of silently entering active scope.

tasks.title: CLI runtime profile wizard add
acceptance_criteria: Provides an ananta init command or equivalent setup wizard.
Detects or asks for local-dev, sandbox or strict runtime mode.
Captures LLM backend preference such as Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI-compatible endpoint or manual config.
Does not require Docker/Podman for local-dev mode.
Writes a reviewable runtime profile file rather than hidden global state.

tasks.title: Hardware and model profile recommendations add
acceptance_criteria: Can recommend conservative defaults for CPU-only, NVIDIA GPU, remote model and mixed local/remote setups.
Represents context window, max input tokens, output tokens, RAG budget and patch size as explicit profile values.
Never silently selects paid/cloud providers without explicit user configuration.
Tests cover RTX-style local GPU, CPU-only and remote OpenAI-compatible profile examples.

tasks.title: Optional deployment target generation add
acceptance_criteria: Can generate optional docker-compose or podman-compatible examples when requested.
Keeps local-dev as non-container default.
Marks sandbox and strict modes clearly as stronger isolation choices.
Does not overwrite existing compose/config files without explicit confirmation or backup.

tasks.title: MCP integration boundary document write
acceptance_criteria: Documents MCP as Hub-controlled integration, not a worker-side tool free-for-all.
Defines read-only first, default-deny, capability-bound tool access.
Explains which MCP operations require approval and audit.
Explicitly prevents MCP tools from bypassing Ananta policy, approval or artifact flows.

tasks.title: MCP tool registry skeleton add
acceptance_criteria: Registers MCP servers/tools as descriptors with capability, risk, read/write/admin class and allowed scopes.
Unknown tools are unavailable by default.
Registry exposes health/degraded states without executing tools.
Tests cover valid descriptor, unknown tool, duplicate tool and unsafe default.

tasks.title: Read-only MCP smoke path add
acceptance_criteria: Implements a read-only/mockable MCP adapter path.
Requires Hub capability and policy decision before use.
Returns artifacts or structured results with provenance.
Does not implement write/admin MCP execution in this task.

tasks.title: PR review agent design add
acceptance_criteria: Documents PR review automation as blocked until native worker, approval, audit and release gate are stable.
Defines review-only behavior first: analyze diff, run allowed checks, produce ReviewArtifact.
Posting comments back to Git providers is separate from code modification.
No auto-merge or direct push behavior is included.

tasks.title: Git provider webhook receiver skeleton add
acceptance_criteria: Accepts signed GitHub/GitLab-style webhook payloads in test mode.
Creates review tasks only when provider, repository and event type are allowed.
Does not run worker execution directly inside the request handler.
Tests cover invalid signature, unsupported event and accepted PR event.

tasks.title: PR ReviewArtifact posting adapter add
acceptance_criteria: Publishes review summaries generated by Ananta as comments only after policy allows outbound integration.
Can run in dry-run mode and produce artifact-only output.
Never posts secrets, raw prompts or unredacted logs.
Tests use mocked Git provider client.

tasks.title: Icebox decision record for heavy client features add
acceptance_criteria: Records why full LSP server work, visual flow builder and large Eclipse refactors are deferred.
Defines conditions required to promote any of them into active scope.
Reinforces thin-client and no-duplicate-orchestration rules.
Prevents accidental scope creep into current release tracks.

tasks.title: Current track inventory and completed-track references add
acceptance_criteria: Lists active todo files and their intended order without replacing the detailed todos.
Records completed and removed todo tracks with evidence pointers.
Distinguishes deferred KRITIS/Enterprise scope from active OSS scope.
Helps future agents avoid rediscovering or duplicating completed tracks.

tasks.title: Cross-track dependency map and validator add
acceptance_criteria: Documents allowed cross-track dependencies using explicit file:id references.
Validator reports missing files, missing task IDs and circular cross-track links.
Unknown or archived track references fail with actionable messages.
Validation can run in CI without requiring network or privileged access.

execution_order_summary: First add runtime profile wizard and profile recommendations after core Security/Domain/Worker tracks are stable enough.
Then add MCP boundary documentation and a read-only/default-deny MCP skeleton.
Keep PR review automation blocked until worker release gates are stable.
Keep LSP, Flow Builder and large Eclipse refactors in the icebox until the OSS golden path is reliable.
Add cross-track dependency mapping and validation so stale references cannot silently drift.

notes: Stale references to missing todo.security.json and todo.ananta-worker.json were replaced with explicit KRITIS gating task references.
Milestone-level execution_stage_summary was added and synchronized with task statuses.
Cross-track dependency mapping/validation was added as PG-T12 to reduce future track drift.
Compatibility stage aliases TEST-M1..TEST-M6 were added for test-track tooling expectations.

summary_notes: This file intentionally avoids duplicating active detailed tracks and now references existing KRITIS gates explicitly.
Runtime profile UX and controlled MCP remain the near-term additions with the best OSS value/risk ratio.
PR review automation remains blocked until audit/mutation/evolver gates in KRITIS are verifiably stable.
Cross-track dependency hygiene is now an explicit work item to prevent stale references.
LSP, Flow Builder and large Eclipse refactors remain icebox items to avoid Medusa complexity.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: todo.json war syntaktisch valide, hatte aber veraltete Cross-Track-Referenzen auf nicht vorhandene Dateien.
Es fehlte ein execution_stage_summary fuer milestonebasierte Rollups.

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: Abhaengigkeiten von PG-T07 wurden auf konkrete KRITIS-Gating-Tasks umgestellt.
Scope-Regeln wurden auf vorhandene Track-Dateien im Repository aktualisiert.

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen und Erweiterungen
items: execution_stage_summary und analysis_checks wurden fuer bessere Nachvollziehbarkeit ergaenzt.
PG-T12 fuehrt eine explizite Cross-Track-Dependency-Map plus Validator ein.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Aktueller Stand: 12 Aufgaben (9 todo, 3 blocked, 0 in_progress, 0 done).
